perturbation.original_text: 1. For the purpose s of this Agreement, “Confidential Information ” shall mean any information disclosed by Oceaneering , whether in writing, orally, visually or otherwise, including but not limited to business plans, contractual, engineering, financial, sales, marketing and operational information, product specifications, technical data , trade secrets , know -how, ideas and concepts of Oceaneering or third parties . Confidential Information excludes, however, information which : (i) is or becomes known or available to Recipient without restriction from a source other than Oceaneering with a legal right to disclose th e same to Recipient; (ii) is, or without violating the terms of this Agreement becomes , generally available to the public ; or (iii) is developed by Recipient independently of the information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. For the purpose s of this Agreement, “Confidential Information ” shall mean any information disclosed by Oceaneering , whether in writing, orally, visually or otherwise, including but not limited to business plans, contractual, engineering, financial, sales, marketing and operational information, product specifications, technical data , trade secrets , know -how, ideas and concepts of Oceaneering or third parties . Confidential Information excludes, however, information which : (i) is or becomes known or available to Recipient without restriction from a source other than Oceaneering with a legal right to disclose th e same to Recipient; (ii) is, or without violating the terms of this Agreement becomes , generally available to the public ; (iii) is developed by Recipient independently of the information disclosed hereunder; or (iv) is disclosed by Oceaneering to a competitor of Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding exclusion (iv): information disclosed by Oceaneering to a competitor of Recipient. Section 2 prohibits Recipient from disclosing 'Confidential Information' to any third party, but now 'Confidential Information' excludes information disclosed to Recipient's competitor. Therefore, Recipient is arguably allowed to disclose information Oceaneering disclosed to their competitor, creating a conflict with Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. With respect to all Confidential Informati on disclosed hereunder, Recipient agrees that from and after the date of this Agreement, Recipient shall not: a. use the Confidential I nformation except for purposes of its business relationship with Oceaneering (the “ Authorized Purpose ”); or b. disclose the Confidential I nformation to any third party except : (i) for directors, officers, managers, employees , consultants , contractors and professional advisors of Recipient (collectively its “Representatives”) who need to know the Confidential Information for the Authorized P urpose and who are subject to an existing obligation to, or enter into an agreement with, Recipient not to disclose Confidential Information ; and (ii) as may be authorized in writing in advance by an officer of Oceaneering .
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds exclusion (iv) to the definition of Confidential Information, stating that information disclosed by Oceaneering to a competitor of the Recipient is *not* considered Confidential Information. Section 2, however, states that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party. Therefore, under the new definition, the Recipient *can* disclose information Oceaneering disclosed to their competitor, directly contradicting the prohibition in Section 2. This is a clear and impactful contradiction, as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Oceaneering grants no rights in or to the Confident ial Information . All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Oceaneering grants a limited, non-exclusive right to use the Confidential Information for internal research purposes only. All Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Oceaneering.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states 'Oceaneering grants no rights'. The modified clause grants a 'limited, non-exclusive right to use' for internal research, creating a direct contradiction. This impacts Section 8, which dictates the return or destruction of all Confidential Information upon written request. The granted right conflicts with the obligation to return *all* information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Upon Oceaneering’s written request, Recipient shall promptly : (i) deliver to Oceaneering and cease to use all Confidential Information in Recipient’s (including its Representatives’) possession, custody or control ; or (ii) destroy the same and delete all electronic records containing the Confidential Information, provided that Oceaneering may require Recipient to certify in writing such destruction and deletion.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Oceaneering grants no rights', but the changed text grants a 'limited, non-exclusive right to use', creating a direct contradiction. This directly contradicts Section 8, which requires the recipient to 'cease to use all Confidential Information' upon request. The changed text explicitly allows use for internal research, while Section 8 demands complete cessation of use, creating a clear inconsistency and in-text contradiction. The permission to use the information directly clashes with the obligation to destroy or return all of it, leading to an unenforceable term.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agre ement sets forth the complete and exclusive statement of the parties’ agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be waived or modified except pursuant to a written agreement signed by the parties. Any waiver or forbearanc e on one occasion shall have no effect on any other occasion.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agre ement sets forth the complete and exclusive statement of the parties’ agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be modified verbally by an officer of Oceaneering. Any waiver or forbearanc e on one occasion shall have no effect on any other occasion.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the agreement may not be modified except by a written agreement signed by both parties. The change allows verbal modification by an officer of Oceaneering, directly contradicting the written modification requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agre ement sets forth the complete and exclusive statement of the parties’ agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be modified verbally by an officer of Oceaneering. Any waiver or forbearanc e on one occasion shall have no effect on any other occasion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified by a written agreement signed by both parties. The changed text allows verbal modification by an officer of Oceaneering. The contradicted text is the changed text, which now directly contradicts the original intent of requiring written modification, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as it creates conflicting terms regarding how the contract can be modified.
